VERIFIED via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) Binding (registry.js:188), requirements (registry.js:2006), no-write attestation (registry.js:326), descriptor + case-set builders (service.js:3211,3243), Hermes/Claude/handoff packets (service.js:3338,3353,3372), validators (validators.js:788,2100) and scripts/validate-rp21-admin-console-contract.mjs all reference CP00-656 consistently: range RP21.P06.M05.S16-RP21.P06.M06.S03, unit_count=10, risk A, upstream CP00-655, next CP00-657 / RP21.P06.M06.S04. (2) Coverage matches the baseline exactly: deliverable distribution security_audit 4 / test 4 / implementation 2, and micro-title counts Permission And Audit Binding 7 (M05 S16-S22) / Synthetic Fixture Set 3 (M06 S01-S03), re-counted from manifest included_units. validateAdminConsoleCp656Coverage and validateAdminConsoleCp656PermissionAuditFixtureTransitionDescriptor are asserted valid in the validate script (lines 280,292) and the test suite (model.test.js:863-894). (3) No-write/no-real-data boundaries enforced multiple ways: freezeCp656Result (service.js:542) hardcodes ~35 boundary flags to false; required_no_leak_guards present; nested permission_audit_fixture_transition_contract / test_evidence_contract / runtime_boundary checks (validators.js:2186-2233) confirm evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_permission_decision, emits/persists_audit_event, fixture_payload_included, real_tenant/user/matter_data_loaded, exposes_unauthorized_count, fixture_runtime_opened, permission_matrix_runtime_opened and hermes_runtime_opened all false. (4) Authority boundary preserved: Claude review packet read_only:true, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2:true; Hermes appears only as deterministic evidence-packet labels (H21.CP00-656...) with hermes_runtime_opened:false / runtime_receipt_emitted:false - not product runtime. (5) Honest state: manifest production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / status in_progress pending this review, construction-inspection verdict PENDING, adjudication production_ready:no - no premature production_ready claim. (6) Plan/queue consistent: live_latest_pack_id CP00-656, live_next_unit_id RP21.P06.M06.S04, next queue pack CP00-657; contract current_pack and generated_from_pack_id both CP00-656; source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65... matches across baseline/manifest/plan/queue. command-evidence.json reports all required validations (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, rp21:admin-console:validate, build) at exit_code 0; these are self-attested but corroborated by inspectable source (regenerated contract deep-matches the binding/descriptor per the validate script, tests present and asserting the claimed boundaries, validators wired). review-request/readiness confirm read-only Read,Grep,Glob, model claude-opus-4-8, effort max; auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, non-zero stdout) with none of the forbidden review-evidence conditions (not_logged_in, malformed_json, quota_failure, permission_denied, tool_call_only_output). Pack is closeout-eligible at the descriptor layer; this receipt does not assert runtime readiness or enterprise-trust, and human final approval for enterprise trust remains outside this receipt per the stated authority boundary.","findings":[{"id":"CP656-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Top-level no-write false-flag validation loop is tautological (safe-by-construction redundancy)","file":"packages/admin/src/validators.js","line":2183,"details":"validateAdminConsoleCp656PermissionAuditFixtureTransitionDescriptor (validators.js:2146-2185) iterates a falseFlags list of ~35 boundary flags (e.g. writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, emits_audit_event, creates_database_rows) and pushes an error when descriptor[flag] !== false. However, every descriptor is produced by freezeCp656Result (service.js:542-588), which spreads ...result and then hardcodes all of these same flags to false. The top-level check therefore can never fail and would coerce (not detect) a hypothetical future descriptor literal that set one of these flags true at top level. Informational only: the descriptor is safe-by-construction, and the meaningful enforcement remains via the nested checks on permission_audit_fixture_transition_contract.*, test_evidence_contract.*, and runtime_boundary.* (validators.js:2186-2233), which are NOT overridden by freezeCp656Result. The pattern is shared by CP645-CP655 and does not affect CP00-656 correctness or the descriptor-only / no-real-data guarantees.","recommendation":"Optional cross-pack hardening: drop the redundant top-level loop, or assert the false-flags on the pre-freeze builder input so the check validates rather than coerces. No action required for CP00-656 closeout.","post_review_disposition":"Non-blocking informational. Descriptor is safe-by-construction (flags hardcoded false in freezeCp656Result); the nested contract and runtime_boundary checks provide the real coverage.
